# Fixture provenance — pptx/stress

12 PPTX fixtures sourced from upstream Apache POI + python-pptx test
corpora plus one OSS-Fuzz crash repro. These exercise the *abnormal*
cases the reader has to survive — minimal / no-slides / no-core-props
decks, missing-rels files, comments, charts, SmartArt, tables, and an
OSS-Fuzz crash minimal reproducer. See the audit commit
`2ad3631` ("Add PPTX extraction design docs, format reference, and
stress test fixtures", 2026-04-02) which documents the sourcing.

| File | Source | License | Retrieved | SHA-256 | Notes |
|------|--------|---------|-----------|---------|-------|
| SmartArt.pptx | Hand-crafted minimal SmartArt deck (creator `user`, 2021-11-10) used as a SmartArt OPC-fallback reproducer. Vendored alongside the Apache POI stress corpus. | `<unknown — needs verification>` (small SmartArt repro file; precise upstream URL not recorded — likely from python-pptx / Apache POI / a public SmartArt-extraction bug report) | 2026-04-02 | b97e4c6d2ee1dd4094f50f9043820610268dd452d7717c537f5456636adcc353 | Only Python tool that extracts SmartArt text via `diagrams/data1.xml` OPC fallback — this fixture pins that behaviour. |
| bar-chart.pptx | Apache POI test corpus (`apache/poi` test resources, originally `My Pie Chart` by `yegor`, lastModifiedBy `Alain Béarez` who is an Apache POI committer). | Apache-2.0 (Apache POI test resources) | 2026-04-02 | 79e1d218bfb2903e8dc8425a6b1997d9c1976f5a5f025bada85b0c47b5777969 | Chart-as-Table linearization. |
| clusterfuzz-minimal.pptx | OSS-Fuzz minimal-crash reproducer for a PPTX parser (no `docProps/core.xml`, deliberately malformed minimal PPTX). | `<unknown — needs verification>` (OSS-Fuzz reproducer files are typically released as public-domain / CC0 by the OSS-Fuzz program; confirm before redistribution) | 2026-04-02 | 27fb0d0641003899ab36e96e1aa1811eac43c5e1ac496a148b9d1f217db31ffc | Crash-resistance fixture — the reader must not segfault / hang on this file. |
| comment.pptx | Inter-American Development Bank (IADB) — "Water Finance" presentation by Antonio Vives (1998–2002, lastModifiedBy `XPVMWARE01`). Distributed publicly by IADB; surfaced by the Apache POI / python-pptx maintainers as a comment-extraction reproducer. | `<unknown — needs verification>` (IADB publication; original distribution license not asserted in the file metadata. IADB content is generally distributed under CC-IGO licences but the specific terms for this 1998 deck need to be confirmed.) | 2026-04-02 | 0295a51f63150e3ee680154d07dab88062ba2306030b790561f85436f3ded7c8 | Real comment-extraction fixture (largest in this directory at ~308 KB). |
| grouping_issues.pptx | IBM test deck (creator `Timothy Walter Lai`, Company `IBM`, 2012). Used by python-pptx / Apache POI to reproduce a group-shape recursion bug. | `<unknown — needs verification>` (IBM-authored deck; original distribution channel not recorded. Likely public — surfaced by a python-pptx bug report — but warrants confirmation before any re-release.) | 2026-04-02 | a8639e12e763a01d889a6178e154c21db2dd4275f8f35ac81a458f81e26b2c18 | Group-shape recursion path. |
| minimal.pptx | python-pptx test corpus — `Presentation` (creator `python-pptx`, lastModifiedBy `Steve Canny`, Company `neopraxis.org`). Smallest valid PPTX file generated by python-pptx. | MIT (python-pptx — `github.com/scanny/python-pptx` `LICENSE`) | 2026-04-02 | f04f94947620332c06dd2ad3c1aa52cb614d3b8d608e8d6fcbe66f5a29c5e4ee | Smoke-test fixture. |
| missing_rels_item.pptx | python-pptx test corpus — variant of `minimal.pptx` with a `.rels` entry deliberately removed. | MIT (python-pptx test resources) | 2026-04-02 | 9a156f00b5faeaf15d12ea44bc49dc8f0c5dd7411352445b4908630bc6a2db28 | Reader must surface a clean error, not a cryptic KeyError. |
| no-core-props.pptx | Cisco Systems "Empty Default White PowerPoint Presentation" (Company `Cisco Systems, Inc.`). `docProps/core.xml` is absent — exercises the no-core-props read path. | `<unknown — needs verification>` (Cisco-issued empty presentation; surfaced by python-pptx / Apache POI corpus. Reader-test only — content is effectively a blank template.) | 2026-04-02 | 93289b5e951a7256ef783651e1245284472c77512fc3d8df17680dd693fc5c6c | Pins reader behaviour when core props are missing. |
| no-slides.pptx | python-pptx test corpus — "Empty Default White PowerPoint Presentation" (creator + lastModifiedBy `Steve Canny`). Valid OPC package with zero slides. | MIT (python-pptx test resources) | 2026-04-02 | 76b8a109c450fedce7a44bf03b385a327ad0bf99d583e492fa49b760860a77db | Reader must return an empty document, not crash. |
| pie-chart.pptx | Apache POI test corpus (`apache/poi` test resources, creator `yegor`, lastModifiedBy `Yegor Kozlov` — an Apache POI committer). | Apache-2.0 (Apache POI test resources) | 2026-04-02 | 3b6404b59b24cb79fbb91fc2e92bd8b80cdc340aeed71a1ec1e267db0d8ad444 | Pie-chart linearization. |
| table_test.pptx | Apache POI test corpus (creator + lastModifiedBy `Andreas Beeker` — Apache POI committer "kiwiwings", 2015). | Apache-2.0 (Apache POI test resources) | 2026-04-02 | b9c3eb4b198ef3e53a4ff1a5ef4118a5ce6ba1760592c553abf665b37dcac5f8 | Simple table coverage. |
| table_test2.pptx | Apache POI test corpus (creator + lastModifiedBy `Andreas Beeker`, 2016). | Apache-2.0 (Apache POI test resources) | 2026-04-02 | e80d09a5fa767f6ee2d024f3a4198f1e8b3241cc638183e5ebafdbacf1fb2838 | Used as the canonical table-with-merge fixture in `tests/unit/test_pptx_reader.py` `test_simple_table`. |

## TODO (human verification)

The rows below have provenance that needs a confirmed upstream URL and
license assertion. None of these files contain customer / privileged
material — the open items are about license attribution hygiene, not
about whether the fixture is safe to ship in a public test corpus.

- `SmartArt.pptx` — pin the upstream URL (python-pptx issue tracker?
  Apache POI? a public SmartArt-extraction blog repro?).
- `clusterfuzz-minimal.pptx` — confirm the OSS-Fuzz reproducer URL +
  CC0 / public-domain distribution status.
- `comment.pptx` — confirm IADB publication URL and whether the
  1998-vintage deck falls under a CC-IGO licence or another
  distribution channel.
- `grouping_issues.pptx` — confirm the IBM-authored deck's
  public-distribution channel (likely a python-pptx GitHub issue).
- `no-core-props.pptx` — confirm the Cisco template's distribution
  channel; if the fixture is purely structural (no real Cisco
  content), consider replacing with a hand-crafted equivalent to
  remove the third-party attribution question entirely.

## Regenerating

```bash
cd kaos-office
sha256sum tests/fixtures/pptx/stress/*.pptx
# update SHA-256 column above + the matching `Retrieved` date
# (git log -1 --format=%cI -- tests/fixtures/pptx/stress/<file>)
```
